I don't even know which one I have installed on my computer because I've had it for years and years, but it has tons of books included in it and it has tons of different stuff that you can look up really fast. That's a tool that we didn't have years ago that makes things so nice. If you want a copy of scripture to throw it somewhere, you just copy and paste it out of there.

BEN SETTLE:  Imagine that. They used to have to hand write all this stuff, and they had to do it very carefully one stroke of the pen at a time when they were copying it. Now we just copy and paste it.

TERRY DEAN:  We just copy and paste, and if you want to find out what the passage is, you put your little section into the concordance and it finds it for you. Then you copy and paste the passage out. You can click another couple little links and it shows you all the background information on the passage. That's some big advantages that we have today, yet it seems that people know less about the Bible than they used to.

BEN SETTLE:  It's less work now. It's more passive. I'm sure when you were in school, and when I was in school, we didn't even have the internet until my last year in high school. When you did a research paper you had to get the note cards and you had to go through books and you had to look up the things you were going to write about and write each note separately on the note cards. You had to actually learn something.

TERRY DEAN:  Right, and we have other things. I have a Kindle and I have a Bible on my little Kindle. Now if you want to know what a Kindle's for, you put a Bible on it with all your study books, and now you know what purpose a Kindle has. Bible study books are huge. Study Bibles are huge. The Kindle's this little tiny thing and you can carry everything with you. You really see the purpose of a Kindle with the Bible.

BEN SETTLE:  You don't have to bring a Strong's concordance and three study Bibles and the Bible dictionary. Just your little Kindle.

TERRY DEAN:  You just type in the passage you want. You can go to it and do all your stuff on it. That's a great tool. I think my study Bible on the Kindle cost me like $5. We're talking very cheap, very easy to do to use a study Bible. As believers in business, I want people to think about that you are a minister, wherever God's put you, even if you don't tell people about Jesus, even if that's not something that you do all the time. You're still going to live your life, you're still going to be an example, you're still going to be a light that shines, so you need to spend time in the Word for yourself and you need to pray about your business. I have days that I do phone coaching with clients. Those mornings I always start out praying for my clients and that God would give me wisdom in speaking to my clients. If you have that attitude with God, He's going to share it with you. You basically lead your business by what God would want. That's something that we all grow in, we all learn in, and it's a real purpose to have.

The big thing that we already talked about is that people are afraid of loving money or they're afraid of being successful and of business, like it's a bad thing or something along that nature.

Just have the attitude that God wants you to be a blessing. He wants you to be blessed so that you can be a blessing to others, that you can affect others, that you can really reach others, and think of your business that way also. Here's something that's kind of hard for me to say, but I've had at least a dozen emails like this, with people saying that they wanted to do something online or they wanted to help their business and they were praying about it. Then they went and searched on the web and they found me. I've had at least a dozen emails like that over the past few years. If you take that kind of attitude and believe, it puts a responsibility in your business also. It gives you a feeling of responsibility that this isn't just a business. This isn't just a way to provide for my family. This is a ministry and I need to treat this business like a ministry. I care about my customers, I care about my clients, I care about their welfare and what they get out of this, because no matter what service or product I provide, am I a business that God could recommend?

BEN SETTLE:  There's a good question.

TERRY DEAN:  That's a good question to think about. Are you a business that God could recommend? We all talk about referrals. That's the ultimate referral. And with that even, are you a business that treats your customers in a way that other Christians should be referring their friends to you, because that's how He's going to do it. God's going to refer people to your business through others. Are you the kind of business that God would refer because of the way you take care of your customers, the way you treat customers, and the answer there for most of us is, "I don't know," and that's where it comes into the ethical dilemma of are we doing everything we can? That's something I believe that we should never lose. We should never lose the attitude, "Am I doing everything I should as a Christian?" That's kind of a note that we could come near the end of as Christian business people. We should never lose that attitude.

BEN SETTLE:  That's really good. How does spiritual warfare affect Christian business owners, from what you've seen and what you've studied and experienced?

TERRY DEAN:  First of all, let me give you my take on spiritual warfare. Paul only told us to fight one fight. He told us to fight the good fight of faith. That's the only fight he ever told us to fight. He never told us to fight principalities or powers or any of the powers of the air. He mentions them and talks about putting on the whole armor of God, and the whole armor of God is all stuff that revolves around faith also. It talks about peace. It talks about our righteousness. It talks about the Word in the armor of God. Our real fight is a fight of faith, nothing else. I know some Christians have an attitude that they're fighting something "out there." They're fighting the devil or they're fighting demons or they're fighting something that's trying to stop them, whatever they're fighting. But the truth is no matter what would want to stop you out there, Jesus already beat it. We are more than conquerors because He already took care of all those problems, all the powers in this world that would be against you. He already took care of them at the cross. He beat them. They've lost. They've failed. So since nothing else in this world has power over you because of what Jesus did at the cross, nothing has power over us. God could have power to make us do whatever He wants, but part of His love for us is giving us the free choice to do what we want. He doesn't make us do things. He leads us, He guides us, but He doesn't force us. If something is forcing you, then you have an addiction in you. It isn't God. That's something to keep in mind.

With the fight of faith, that means that since there's nothing out there that can control us or beat us, if we fail, that means there's only one side that's responsible. It's us. It might be because we should have never gone that direction in the first place. I told you I've had lots of failures, but they never stopped me. It might be because God never led us that direction. It might be because we didn't take a stand of faith, because no matter what we do, no matter what we're called to do, there's going to be obstacles. There's going to be something that gets in the way. It's never like an easy path. "Hey, if I'm on God's path it'll all be easy and there will never be anything in the way." Look at Paul's life and you'll see that was not true. "I was shipwrecked and I was in the ocean…and then I was in prison." You take Paul's daily routine and it wasn't always easy. Following God isn't always easy, but the fight of faith means that you keep on going anyway. You keep on having faith.

When I first started out, I started praying that God would give me wisdom and I believed that if I prayed for wisdom that would bring the money. That doesn't mean the money came the next day. It didn't show up as a check. It took time. It took me going through failures. It means that when you hit those failures, those mistakes, the things that don't work, you still have faith in what God is doing. I'll tell you this, this is a huge factor in whether people succeed online or not, and that is whether they have confidence and the desire to keep moving, even when failure gets in their way. That's a big key aspect of faith also. When you talk about spiritual warfare, that's where I move into first, is there's nothing out there that can stop me. There's nothing out there that can get in my way. There's things out there that can influence me, but there's nothing out there that can stop me. It's all on whether I stand on faith, I stand on God's Word, and I move forward in whatever He's led me to do. That's really how I see spiritual warfare more than anything else, is on that aspect of standing strong in the faith that God has for His business people. That's where it's at.

Your business is going to come under trial. Your business is going to have problems. As I mentioned earlier, Job had tons of trials at one point, and he had a business there, since he was the richest man. He had a business and he went through tons of trials through this and eventually gained it back. It doesn't mean that your business is always going to be successful. You might have a point that you go through with a whole bunch of trials. Maybe you're losing more money than you're making for a period of time, but you're still going in God's direction. That will take a strong stand of faith for you to get through and move through. Just expect at some point in time you're going to have those types of situations. That's where I really see the spiritual

warfare going on. There's whole different groups when you talk about things like spiritual warfare. I think Christians can almost run themselves in circles because they're trying to fight demons or something else that's out there, instead of taking a stand in faith and stand on what God's doing and just moving forward.

Again, take a look at where Jesus went. Wherever Jesus was, He had authority. Wherever Jesus went, He was in control. You'll notice that when they put Him on the cross, the one thing He did was it said He spoke not a word. You'll notice that's the only time that Jesus was not in control was when He took our place. For the rest of His life, He was in control. For Him not to be in control in that circumstance, He had to keep His mouth shut. That's how much wisdom was in Him. That's how much He had control over whatever situation He was in. He had to purposely basically keep His mouth shut and keep Himself out of the situation for Him to take our place. It's the same thing if you look at Paul going through his life. A passage that really sticks out to me with Paul is when Paul's sitting there in prison and he's discussing what's going to happen to him. As you read through it he's saying, "Should I go home to be with the Lord, which is better to me, or should I stay with you?" I want people to go and read that passage and read it with the mentality, "Why is Paul asking whether it's better for him to go or to stay when he's sitting in prison and they're saying they're going to hang him? Who's in control of that situation?" Paul's discussing this in his mind about whether it's better for him to go home or to stay. He's like, "I don't know which one's better," and then he decides, "I'm going to stay." He ended up getting out of that prison. He ended up not being put to death, and I fully believe that if he would have made the other decision, "It's better for me to go home," that would have been the end right there for him. Even in prison with them saying they're going to kill him, he was still the one in control of the circumstances, and that changes your attitude about a lot of things. No matter what circumstances we're in, we still, with God's help, control the ending. Go read that passage sometime. I can't remember which book it is. He's saying, "Is it more needful for you that I stay, or should I go home?" and think about that from the spiritual warfare side. Think about everything around saying, "I'm going to kill you tomorrow," and Paul's sitting there going, "Should I let them or not?" That's basically what he's doing there. "I don't know. Should I give up and go home?"

How many of us have had that situation when something's hard. "Should I give up or not?" But there was nothing in Paul's attitude about him having any chance of not being in control there, of God not being in control of his circumstance. I wouldn't even say it's that decision for him, because at that point in time he actually said, "It's better for me to go," so it's not him deciding if he's going to heaven or not. It's almost like God gave him the decision. I think in a lot of cases God does give us the choice in our life of what we really want. We have a choice of whether we follow the ministry He has for us or not, and I don't think that affects even our deepest relationship with Him. It does affect our results. It does affect our reward, I'm sure, when we get to heaven, but I'm sure He gives us a choice. At that point in time I think it was more Paul making the decision. "It's just been really hard, and should I just go to heaven now? Should I just let them win right now for the moment, let them finish this ministry right now, or should I continue on?" He decides, "I'm going to continue on." I think Paul has that kind of personality that he just would not quit, and that's an attitude we all need to get. We will not quit! I would say for running our business we have to have that attitude too. We just will not quit. That's a stand of faith that we have in God, that we have in God's plan for us, and that these mistakes won't hold us back and stop us. You'll never find anybody who has a straight path to success. It's always a zig-zagged path.

BEN SETTLE:  Terry, where can people go to find out more about you?

TERRY DEAN:  People can go over to www.MyMarketingCoach.com, where they can find out more about my online training business. You can find my free list that I invite you to sign up for. You'll find my blog, where I give constant free content several times a week, and you can find out about the different products and services I offer and the coaching that I offer for different clients. You'll find all that again at www.MyMarketingCoach.com. If you're interested just in the Christian financial side, I have a Christian financial product at www.NewChristianFinance.com that really has a focus on when I went and started praying for wisdom instead of finances, and goes along with that whole path. It's almost like the other side of the story that I talk about on the internet business side that you'll find at MyMarketingCoach.com, about how I came online and the different things I went through and how I learned to succeed online. NewChristianFinance.com gives you the financial side of that and gives you the Christian side of that and what was going on in my Christian faith during that time. It's like the other part of the story and it really talks a lot about how to get out of debt, how to walk in God's blessing, and how to find God's purpose for you.

BEN SETTLE:  Finally, if somebody reading this is maybe not a Christian yet, maybe they were just reading this book and maybe this is their first exposure to it, or maybe they've been thinking about it - what would you tell them?

TERRY DEAN:  They should pray right now. And here's the big thing about Christians. You know what we accept as Christians? We simply accept that Jesus paid the price for sin. We've all failed. We've all come short of being perfect. That's all you've got to say here, and everyone knows it to be true, that you're not perfect, I'm not perfect, none of us are perfect. We've all failed, yet God is perfect, so that sin separated us from God. Jesus came in the form of flesh. He came and He paid the price for that. He took our separation from God and basically He nailed it to the cross. When they killed Him, He took the death that we deserve. We all deserve death for what we've done, how we've treated people, how we've treated God and others. Jesus paid that price for you. I said early on that I'm delivered from guilt, and that's because He took that place for me. He paid it on the cross for us.

As Christians, all we really do is we accept that He has done that for us, and that we could have never earned God's love. We could have never earned the position in God's family that has been given to us. It's just a free gift of grace that's given to us. Because of that, we accept the forgiveness of our sins. Pray to God and give Him your sin, give Him your life, in exchange for the life that Jesus had and that Jesus gave His life for you.

You'll see tons and tons of commands in the Bible, and you'll hear churches talk about all the rules, but God only gives us two commands - that we love God and that we love our neighbor as ourselves. Everything else hangs on those two laws. Those are the only two laws you have to remember - love God first, and then love your neighbor as yourself. When you pray, ask God to take your sins, take your life, and to help you do that, because that's not always easy and you can't do it without Him. Just like we said that the rich person couldn't go through the eye of a needle, it's impossible, loving God first and loving your neighbor as yourself. That's impossible also for us. We ask for God to do that. That's all that you have to do. Pray to God to come in and change your life, to make you a new person, a new creature, that you're going to follow after Him.

Then after you pray this prayer you're changed. Most people even feel guiltless when they make this prayer. You don't have to feel anything, but most people do feel that. As I said, you don't have to. Then at this point in time I really recommend that if you have any friends that you know are Christians, talk to them. Give them a call and let them know. If they're Christians they should be very happy about what you did, or if you have any family members. A lot of times you're going to find that people who come to the Lord this way, they've had family members praying for them. They'll be very happy for you to give them a call and let them know. You can know which ones have been praying for you, because they're the crazy people who talk about Jesus when you're around them. Give them a call and let them know!

Then I really recommend that you find a church in your area, not just so you can go sit in church, but a lot of times it's nice to find a bigger church because it helps you to change some of your friends that you hang out with, because sometimes the friends that you're hanging with, we all know from business also, that you often end up like the average of all your friends. That's true about your lifestyle also. It ends up the average of all your friends around you. Sometimes you need to change out some of those friends. I say the big churches, because usually the bigger churches have a bigger group of different people with different activities, and a lot of the bigger churches have tons of activities that likely have hobbies that would match up with you, and would likely have people whose interests would match up with you, and you can make some new friends to go along with the ones you already have. I'm not telling you to turn your back on anybody or anything of that nature, but do add other friends who'd be a good influence on you. That's the next thing that I would do.

BEN SETTLE:  Terry, this has been a great call, man. I really appreciate you doing this.

TERRY DEAN:  You're welcome. It's great to talk about the Lord. It's great to talk about business, and we had a good time.

---

# Thank You for Reading!

For over 700 pages of advanced web marketing tips and secrets, please join our free mailing list at www.BenSettle.com

There you'll find a menu of services, products for sales, and even more articles, podcasts and links that can help you quickly build your business.

---

# STOP! Read This First...

The following book is designed for both beginner and advanced affiliate marketers.

However, if you're a veteran affiliate marketer then the first few chapters will likely not contain anything "new" and you can safely skip over (or skim through) them. Although, I think chapter one is mandatory if you want to get the maximum value from this product. The more advanced techniques are revealed in later chapters and in the bonus tips.

---

# Introduction

Below are excerpts of real emails from real product publishers I have been an affiliate for.

"You and [Guru Marketer] are the only two who have really pushed this so far...you're doing far better than he is."

"They [Big Copywriting Training Company] profiled me in an interview...about a month ago...Got me a ton of opt-ins, but between you and me, you're still pulling in more sales."

"Here are your numbers, dude. You're my best affiliate!"

"According to Clickbank... you're my #1 [affiliate]."

Before reading further, there are a few things you should know:

I am not a "super affiliate."

I have not made a million dollars in affiliate marketing.

And affiliate marketing is not my main "thing."

So why should you bother reading this book?

Three reasons:

1. I have a modest-sized (but ever-growing) list of customers, just like you may have.

2. As the above testimonials show, even with my small list, I am sometimes able to out-sell (or at least out-convert) marketers with much bigger name recognition with lists that dwarf mine in size.

3. The reason I am able to do this is not because I'm some affiliate marketing "guru" or because I am some kind of super copywriter who can sell ice to Eskimos. It's simply because...

I Leverage My List Better Than Most of My Competitors

And that's what this book is really about.

It's not about selling, making money or even affiliate marketing.

It's about leverage.

I like to think of it almost as Marketing "Jeet Kune Do."

Jeet Kune Do (JKD) is a fighting philosophy created by the legendary martial artist, Bruce Lee.

Bruce Lee was not a big guy. In fact, he was small. (He weighed something like 125 pounds.)  And despite what you see in his movies, he did not believe in trading punches or kicks with his opponents. He actually taught his students something quite different altogether: Instead of using your hands and feet in a fight, he'd tell you to use your elbows, knees and the top of your head (the hardest and strongest parts of your body) to attack your opponent's eyes, testicles and face (the softest and most vulnerable parts of his body).

This gives even the slowest and weakest person an enormous amount of leverage in a fight -- even against a fighting powerhouse like, for example, someone as big and mean as ex-boxing champ Mike Tyson.

See, for Bruce Lee, self-defense was not about strength, speed, roundhouse kicks, acrobatics or any of the fancy gimmicks you see in the old "Samurai Sunday" movies.

It was about leverage.

Leveraging your body's natural "heavy artillery" to devastate your opponent --regardless of your own fighting experiences, physical health or coordination. It is almost exactly like pulling an equalizing trump card in a fight -- that lets even 75-year-old grandmas hold their own against bigger, stronger and more skilled attackers.

And that's the concept you're going to learn in this book.

Instead of trading punches with the "Mike Tyson's" of affiliate marketing -- and hoping to get in a sale or two before they roll out the same offer you are to their massive lists -- you will learn how to take your finger and jab them in the eye.

Or knee them in the testicles.

Or smash their face into the top of your head.

(All figuratively speaking, of course!)

In the end, while you won't put anyone out of business, you will "stun" your larger, more cash-flush competitors long enough to sell to the buyers you both share --and make them YOUR customers, instead of theirs.

The result will be converting two, three, maybe even four times as many customers into sales than you are now -- which means depositing two, three even four times more cash in the bank -- regardless of your list size or name recognition now.

Plus, as a pleasant "side effect" of using these methods, you will also simultaneously grow your list much faster (with the following methods, your sites will become a lot more attractive to search engines and generate way more word-of-mouth), while keeping your conversion rates more or less consistent.

Now, one more thing before we get started:

I'm not going to insult your intelligence and guarantee you'll make a million dollars using these secrets. Nor will I say this method is necessarily the "best" way to conduct your affiliate marketing campaigns.

Just think of this book as your trump card when playing for market share against affiliate marketers with bigger names and influence. Or if you simply have a small list and want to just convert the maximum number of people on your list into sales.

Play this "card" right and you can realistically walk away with more traffic, more customers and more money each time you sit down at the table to play -- no matter what kind of hand you are dealt.

---

# Step One: How to "Prep" Your List to Buy From You... And Only You

This first step may seem kind of "touchy feely."

Or even like "fluff" to fill up pages.

Trust me, it's not.

Not only is this the first step of the process in this book, but it's also the most important.

In fact, if you ignore this one (extremely simple) step, you are not going to get the full value from this book. You will get some value from it. But your sales and conversions will be far short of what they could (and should) be.

Frankly, if I had to pick just one affiliate marketing "method" to use, and had nothing else going for me, this step would be it.

Anyway, here's the step:

Sincerely Caring About the People on Your List

Now, I am not saying you don't care about the people on your list.

In fact, I am assuming you do care about them -- and deeply.

But... I am going to suggest a rather unique way of looking at them you may not have considered. A way that has been invaluable to me since throwing my hat in the affiliate marketing game.

You see, I don't believe in the "herd" list mentality that has been popularized on the Internet. This is when you view your list as a "herd" of cattle you've roped and bound in one place (your list) ready to give you money on command when called (i.e., when sending them offers).

I look at my list differently than that.

I don't see a bunch of names, email addresses, cows or a "herd" of anything.

I see people.

Real, flesh-and-blood people with real problems, real goals, real hopes and real dreams. People who maybe spend Sunday evenings dreading the coming week, lay awake at night plagued with insomnia and stress, and who are dealing with very serious fears, problems and insecurities.

I also see them as my family, in a way.

In fact, before I even think of writing an ad or sending out an offer, I picture I'm writing not to a "list"... but to someone I love and care about deeply.

Believe it or not, this small mindset shift changes the entire complexion of the sale.

Instead of thinking only about how to overpower them with big promises and marketing "ballistics", I think about the urgent problem(s) they have and how to help them eliminate it.

And from here on out, I urge you to do the same thing.

If you sell, for example, a product about curing arthritis, then try to picture what it'd be like if your own mother was suffering from the disease. What if she spent 90% of each day in pain and could barely sew, cook or do the things she loves in her retirement years because of the almost crippling pain in her hands?

What would you tell her?

What would you say to her in an email?

What product would you tell her about?

Asking these questions automatically shifts your thinking from being "product oriented" (which always comes off as self-serving) to "problem oriented" (which is always "customer oriented").

It automatically changes the way you interact with your customers. It changes the products you choose to promote. And, most important of all...

It Changes the Way Your List Responds To Your Offers

Trust me on this.

If there's one thing I do "better" than my competitors, this is it.

Fact is, the rest of this book is going to be mostly technique and activity-based instruction. But this first (and most important) step is pure mindset.

Get this part right -- sincerely caring about your list, and thinking about their problems before thinking about selling them something -- and you will stand out head and shoulders from all your competition -- regardless of how big your name, list, and influence is now.

---

# Step Two: What the World's Greatest Salesmen Always Do First

This second step, much like step one, is not "sexy."

No "killer" marketing secrets, "genius" tactics or "amazing" persuasion strategies included.

But it is what the world's greatest salesmen do to sell more products and services faster, easier and in greaternumbers than their competitors.

And it is almost guaranteed to beef up your affiliate conversions, too.

This second step is to always, always always...

Focus On Solutions To Problems

Simple?

You bet!

But don't let that simplicity fool you.

Digging up solutions to the most pressing, "screaming crisis" problems your list has will not only drive up your conversions, but it will make sure you pick products that are in perfectharmony with what your list wants, needs and is willing to pay for.

And, fact is, every time you pick the right product to sell (that is in tune with what your list wants), the more your list will be open to your next offer.

This may seem basic (heck, it is basic). But it's extremely important.

Because if you are only picking the latest guru-of-the-month's product -- just because "everyone else" is promoting it, or there is some jazzed up launch going on -- you will likely get some sales. A lot or a few.

But if it isn't really targeted to your list's current problems -- the thoughts, desires and anxieties keeping them awake on Sunday nights -- they will start to tune you out.

Maybe not right away.

But each time you miss the "mark" here, you are losing their attention bit by bit until, eventually, you will have big chunks of your list (including potential buyers) deleting your emails sight unseen, or who leave your list altogether.

Let me give you an example of what I mean:

One of the very first affiliate products I sold using the methods in this book was John Angelhache's "Copywriting Crash Course" product.

And the reason I picked this product specifically is because it filled a big, gaping want in my list.

You see, writing copy is frustrating for most people.

On my list it was -- and still is for many subscribers -- their #1 concern.

And John's product really solves that hot, urgent problem for people who need to be better copywriters, but are confused, not getting the numbers they want, and don't know where to turn.

The result?

As of this writing, I am the #1 affiliate for that product (even with much bigger names promoting it) -- and it also has a low return rate. In fact, it so matches what my list wants, I sometimes get "thank you" emails from people who paid me to get the product!

And it's all a result of finding a problem my list had first.

You can do the same thing.

After you've shifted your mindset from "what product do I sell" to "how can I better serve my list" (step #1) you will naturally start thinking of problems to solve (step #2), and then looking for products that have those solutions (step #3 -- next).

This is not a "new" concept, by any means.

In fact, it's marketing 101.

And yet, it's so little-used by affiliate marketers, anyone who knows and understands it has a huge head start right from the beginning.

---

# Step Three: How to Find the Perfect Affiliate Product to Sell

If you're on my list you will notice I rarely sell the same "thing" everyone else is.

Reason why is not because I'm some kind of "snob" or rebel who hates conforming to what everyone else is doing. Nor is it because I don't think I can sell those products.

No... it is because of what we talked about in the last step:  Most of those products simply don't solve the urgent problems in the lives of those on my list.

However, that's only part of the reason.

There are times (and products) that very much do solve the problems of my list. But, I know they are getting bombarded with the same pitches over and over from other people. And while I don't always let that stop me, sometimes I focus on something else to sell instead.

Why?  Well, let's go back to our "Bruce Lee mindset."

Besides teaching leverage in battle, there was another thing he taught that applies as much to affiliate marketing as it does fighting:

The Best Way to Win a Fight Is Not By Fighting At All

Here's an example from Bruce Lee's classic movie Enter the Dragon:

There's a scene early in the movie when Bruce's character is on a boat with a bunch of other fighters traveling to a combat tournament on a nearby island. One of the fighters approaches Bruce and picks a fight with him. Bruce recommends fighting on a nearby beach, and that they take a small row boat to it. The other guy agrees and gets on the row boat, with Bruce behind him.

As soon as the guy gets on the row boat, Bruce simply unties the ropes connecting the boat to the main ship they are on, and lets the row boat float out -- essentially leaving the guy stranded in the row boat with no way to get back on the main ship.

The lesson?

Bruce "won" the fight by not fighting in the first place.

Bruce was very proud of that scene. He fully believed that was the best way to win a fight. To him -- perhaps the greatest fighter of his time -- "fighting" was actually a last resort.

And it's very similar with affiliate marketing, too.

At least, it has been for me.

What I like to do -- and what has been extremely effective for me -- is pick products that:

1. Solve a pressing problem.

2. Are high quality.

3. Are overlooked by most everyone else.

4. Have great stories behind them.

This isn't a hard rule by any means.

Sometimes you will want to sell something being promoted by lots of others. And in those cases, the methods in this book and audio lessons will help you stick out like a sore thumb.

But, generally speaking, I believe if you follow these four criteria for picking products to sell you literally cannot fail.

Now, we talked about the pressing problem. And promoting a high quality product should be a "must" no matter what. But choosing products that are overlooked and have great stories can make a huge difference, too.

For example:

One of the products I sell as an affiliate is Ryan Healy's "How To Get Your First Paying Copywriting Client In 14 Days Or Less."

I've known Ryan for a while. I actually interviewed him before he created his freelance copywriting product and already knew -- and was fascinated by -- his story.

You see, Ryan wasn't "handed" anything.

He had no special breaks... no special contacts in the industry... and had a lot of pressure on him to succeed (he had no job and only two weeks of money with a mortgage, a child, a pregnant wife and no insurance -- whoa!)

Needless to say, the story of how he got started making money as a freelance copywriter is both inspirational and valuable for anyone wanting to break into the freelance copywriting business.

Not only did I think this... but so did many of the struggling freelance copywriters on my list.

So it was a total no-brainer to promote his course when it became available.

Sure, there are lots of more popular "how to become a freelance copywriter" courses.

But Ryan's story and methods are much more in "tune" with my list. They can relate a lot more to Ryan's story.

Fact is, many of today's copywriting gurus (and this is no reflection on them, just a natural result of their "celebrity" status) could offer their grocery lists for sale and they'd sell just because they are, well, celebrities.

Plus, Ryan's product has another key element:

It is "different."

In many ways it is actually "new."  And even when he teaches things people have already heard, Ryan has positioned those things in a way where they are new again.

This is extremely powerful. Because as Internet marketing pioneer Ken McCarthy said when I interviewed him for my Copywriting Grab Bagbook:

"New is the most important word in advertising...It may be the oldest thing on earth and has been done a million times. But you got to find the angle that makes it new. And when something is new it immediately cuts through all the clutter. We're wired to respond to new. We're wired to pay attention to new."

Bottom line:  If you want to increase your affiliate marketing conversions and profits, focus on selling products that:

1. Solve a pressing problem your list has.

2. Are high quality.

3. Are overlooked (although, again, not always necessary)

4. Have great stories behind them.

Do this and you almost can't fail.

---

# Step Four: How to Stick Out Like a Sore Thumb From Other Affiliate Marketers

Okay, now that you've picked your product, it's time for the next step...

Buying Your Own, Unique Domain Name

Why bother doing this?

Why not just use the affiliate link you're given when you sign up as an affiliate?

Well, for one thing, many people have "hang ups" about buying from affiliate links.

Maybe they think they are paying more, or maybe they just don't like the idea of someone getting a commission from what they buy.

Who knows?

But the fact is, a lot of people (I have heard as many as 30% or more) will go out of their way to not buy from an affiliate link.

Just the mere fact you have your own domain name will cut down what many affiliate marketers call "affiliate hijacking."  This is when someone:

1. Sees your affiliate link, signs up as an affiliate and buys the product themselves (at a discount) or...

2. Finds the original sales letter and buys the product directly from the product owner -- even though you did all the work of attracting and pre-selling that lead. (By the way, if you use the steps in this book, this will almost never be a problem).

Another reason to have your own domain name is because it's much easier to remember a unique domain name than an affiliate link.

For example, what's easier to remember:

http://vantagenet.crejoker.hop.clickbank.net

Or...

So with that in mind, here's what you do:

1. Pick a domain registrar

Right now, I use Host Gator for my domain names.  But you can find all kinds of domain companies online.

Just remember:  You get what you pay for.

I used to have some of my domains with a company that had dirt-cheap prices... but I'd have a lot of problems with them.

In fact, one of them actually cost me money in lost sales.

I won't pretend to understand the technical details behind these things -- but I have regretted it every time I've cheaped-out on buying domain names.

2. Pick a ".com" name that is easy to remember andcompatible with what you sell.

For example:

One of the products I sell as an affiliate is Jay White's "Catalog Copywriting Secrets" course.

When I was searching for a domain, I knew I'd need something that would seep right into a person's mind -- with no chance to misread, forget or misspell it.

I also wanted something that conveyed making money to keep it benefit-oriented.

So I went with "www.CatalogCashMachine.com".

See what I mean?

Simple, easy to remember, and gets the message across.

Other examples of domain names that are easy for the mind to "digest" I've chosen include:

- SwipeFileSecrets.com
- GetCopywritingClients.com
- SecretCopywritingTactics.com
- PublicitySystem.com

Now, isn't it clear -- at least in essence -- what those are selling just by their respective domain names?

That's the effect you want your domain names to have, too.

You want to make the entire ordering process -- even as far back as the domain name -- as easy to "digest" for your customers as possible.

3. Point your domain name to your affiliate link.

You can do this right through your domain name company's control panel.

Since every domain name company is different, I refer you to the instructions you're given when you purchase your domain.

Just go to their "help desk" section and type in the words, "domain forwarding."

Incidentally, there are also many link "cloaking" programs you can try, too.

I have not used any of them myself.

But if I was going to use a link cloaking program, I would go with the "Affiliate Link Cloaker" program -- www.AffiliateLinkCloaker.com -- as I've heard quite a few good things about them.

---

# Step Five: How to Almost Force People to Buy From You... Even If They Don't Want the Product You're Selling

This is a fairly simple step that will almost always get people on your list buying from you over and above other affiliates.

Chances are you will never be the only other affiliate selling a product.

If the people on your list are also on the lists of other affiliate marketers selling the exact same product you are, then it definitely pays to stick out from those other marketers as much as possible.

And if you are competing against marketers with more name recognition and "celebrity" status than you, then the cards are stacked against you before you even begin.

The solution?

Offer Unique and Valuable Bonuses to Your Customers.

Bonuses people on your list want so badly they simply have to order from you -- if for no other reason than to get the bonuses.

This is where many affiliate marketers get lazy and end up leaving money on the table.

Instead of taking the time and energy to create (or find) "for real" valuable and unique bonuses, they offer books or reports or other bonuses people already have ten copies of -- or can download free somewhere.

No good.

At least, not if you want to fully leverage your list.

Instead, it's much better to either create or find truly unique and valuable bonuses that eliminate urgent problems in your customers' lives.

Trust me, this part may take a little effort -- but it's well worth it.

Not only will your refund rates plummet when you do what I am about to suggest, but you will find customers buying from you again and again just to get the bonuses!

So what's the trick to finding bonuses?

Well, remember what we talked about before -- focusing on solutions to problems. So the first thing is to only choose bonuses that are going to solve an urgent, pressing problem.

You basically have two choices:

1. Find bonuses

2. Create your own bonuses

Either one is easy if you know what to do.

The key is to find or create something so valuable it either sells on its own (or has sold on its own in the past), or could be sold as a product independently of your main offer.

For example:

- You can write a special report solving a very specific (and painful ) problem your customers have.
- You can put on a tele-seminar and offer the recordings as a bonus.
- You can interview other experts in your niche and offer the interview recordings and/or transcripts.
- You can create a video that shows your customers how to solve a problem. (Both audio and video are great because they have high perceived value.)

And so on, and so forth.

The important thing here is not so much the way you package your bonuses... but that they solve a hot, pressing problem (or fulfill a passionate desire) your customers have.

You want them strategically packed with credibility, benefits and solutions to problems your customers are likely to have. This will add a tremendous amount of value to the deal. Far more value than the main product you are selling alone.

Again, this goes back to sincerely caring about your customers. Whatever their main problems are, you have to think about them and put yourself in their shoes.

What will make their lives easier?

What will remove pain from their lives?

What do they want?

Answer these questions and you can create high value bonuses for your customers all day long.

Another option is to license products to offer as bonuses. You can find licenses for certain products ranging anywhere from free to thousands of dollars.

Or, if you really want to save time, pull your bonuses from other products. Just take chapters or parts of other info products -- that you created or that someone else created (with their permission, of course) -- and use those as bonuses.  Big direct mailing houses -- like Rodale Books, for example -- do this with great success. They will find chapters of books they used to sell (or still sell) and repackage them as stand-alone reports and premiums. It's a very fast and effective way to add more items to your arsenal of bonuses.

Also -- and we'll talk more about this in the next step -- you want to make sure at least one or two of your bonuses are from your own products. That way it's like a "double whammy" -- they add a ton of value to your offer and help you pre-sell the product(s) you took your content from at the same time.

I do this with several of the affiliate products I sell, and the back ends sales are essentially "free money" I didn't have to do a lick of work for.

If you want to use other peoples' content, you will obviously have to get their permission. Which means you will have to contact them.

But don't worry, there's no need to get all "sale letter-y" on them.

Just write a simple email (or snail mail letter) explaining who you are, what you are wanting to do, and the benefits of letting you use their content as bonuses for your affiliate marketing offers.

I have even reprinted a template of an email I've used to secure bonuses here for you:

Email Template for Securing Bonuses for Your Products

You have full permission to use this template, modify it, and make any changes you need to it.

Hey [NAME],

My name is [YOUR NAME], I know we've never met, but I was hoping I could ask a favor of you.

I was wondering if I could use a [CHAPTER/SECTION/PART] of your [THEIR PRODUCT] as a bonus for a [TYPE OF PRODUCT] I'm selling about [SUBJECT OF YOUR PRODUCT].

Specifically, the [SECTION/CHAPTER/PART] about [SECTION/CHAPTER/PART SUBJECT].

This is just an incredible tip I've used profitably several times now.

Ideally, I'd like to use the entire [SECTION/CHAPTER/PART] while giving full credit to you at the end - promoting anything you'd like.

While I don't have a crystal ball -- I'm confident this product will sell well, in which case you'd get some valuable traffic (buyers of an [TYPE OF PRODUCT] product) from those seeing your tips.

Anyway, if it'd be okay to use that as a bonus, please let me know when you get a chance.

If, on the other hand, you'd rather pass on this, I totally understand, it's no problem at all. I realize you have to protect your content and probably don't want just anyone using it for just any product.

Either way, I want to thank you for all that you do. I enjoy your teachings and find them extremely valuable.

[YOUR NAME]

P.S. If you have any questions, call me at [YOUR PHONE NUMBER]. If you want to know if I'm a straight shooter and will do right by you, we have a number of friends and contacts in common, including [NAMES OF PEOPLE YOU BOTH KNOW].

As a customer of this book, you have full permission to use it:

Just "plug" in your details and you're good to go.

Whatever the case, I have done this many times and have never had a problem.

After all, if you're dealing with smart marketers (and why waste time with anyone else?) they will almost always happily agree -- as they get free publicity and a possible sale (if the material you pick is truly valuable). And you get to offer unique and valuable content.

It's a true "win-win."

Finally, another way to gather valuable bonuses is by looking for PLR (Private Label Rights) material for sale. This is where you buy the rights to use someone else's material and rework it however you want -- with your name, contact info, format, extra content, packaging, etc. It's essentially "your" content, even though you didn't do anything to create it.

A Few More "Fine Points" About Bonuses

- To deliver your bonuses, simply tell your customers to email you their product receipt. This is "manual labor", but I have not yet found another way to do this. Especially with ClickBank and other affiliate marketing programs where you are not automatically notified by email of a sale. Plus, this also helps you get to know and even bond with your customers better.
- Deliver more bonuses than you advertise. This makes your customers feel real good about buying from you. It also makes people more likely to want to buy from you again. After all, everyone loves a business that over delivers.
- Finally, make sure you protect your bonuses. You can either use a password protected page (your web host company can show you how to set one up). Or you can use special software like dlguard.com.

Either way of protecting your content is fine. The point is to keep the search engines from "spidering" your bonuses for the whole world to see. In our next step, you'll see how to further monetize your bonuses.

---

# Step Six: How to Automatically Double (Even Triple) Your Profits on Each Sale

One detail that seems to get lost on marketers today -- even experienced marketers who should "know better" -- is the real money is not in the upfront sale.

It's In Your Back End Sales

That's one of the reasons why the billion dollar direct mail industry is more than willing to break even -- or take a loss -- on their front end products. Because they know they'll get you on the back-end with something else.

And it's no different with affiliate marketing.

Each and every sale you make as an affiliate marketer should lead to multiple back-end sales.

The good news is this can be done very easily if you structure your unique bonuses as outlined in the last step.

Here's a real life example:

When someone buys Ryan Healy's "how to get a client" report from my affiliate link, I encourage them to send me their receipt so I can send them a pile of valuable (and highly relevant) bonuses.

As soon as they send me their receipt, I email them a link to a password protected website where they can access several (mostly exclusive) products and links. Among those bonuses are a handful of interviews, reports and resources that -- while quite valuable on their own -- also lead them to other products I sell.

So while I make a modest $22 (give or take) per sale for Ryan's product, I sometimes make an extra $40, $50, even $100 on the back-end!

It's the same with John Angel's swipe file product. I send customers to a page  stuffed with valuable bonuses -- one of which happens to be a discount on a swipe file sold by someone else. About one out of every 10 customers (give or take) buys that product -- turning a $22 sale into a $60 sale. Again, I did no extra "work" to get that sale. It is all automatic.

Of course, most customers won't buy anything else. At least not right away. But there is a certain percentage who do.

And those extra sales don't "cost" you anything in terms of time or energy.

Plus, if you do it the way I have described, those back-end sales often lead to other bonuses and premiums that lead them to other products you sell, which will lead to others, which will lead to others...

And so on, and so forth.

Always remember:  It's not about the one time sale. It's about multiple sales, all strategically linked to each other.

I know this may sound almost too basic. But it's the fundamentals that will put far more money in your pocket than the latest fad or technique-of-the-month. And it will let your one sale multiply and reproduce itself over and over again -- basically on "auto-pilot."

---

# Step Seven: How to Get Product Authors to Do All Your "Selling" For You

The next step is to interview the creator of the product you are selling as an affiliate.

This will not always be possible. In fact, some product owners are downright elitist about this sort of thing. But most product authors want to sell more products and will appreciate you wanting to interview them. After all, it's free publicity for them and is going to help them sell more of their products while adding names to their customer list.

In fact, many times they will even want a copy of the interview for themselves!

However, if this happens, make sure they understand they cannot alter the interview in any way. Reason why is because you want your affiliate link (i.e. your unique domain name) plugged in the interview (more on that in a second).

Now, let's talk about how to do interviews. There's definitely a "science" to this. And the more you learn about it, the more money you will make. Period.

First off, doing interviews isn't nearly as hard as it may seem.

For one thing, you do not need to be a naturally gifted speaker (I'm not) or have an extroverted personality (I certainly don't have one). In fact (and this may seem odd), the more you talk, the less effective your interview will be.

Here are the simple steps to doing your interviews:

1. Get a copy of the product. Go through it, take notes and, if possible, actually USE the product. Get a good feel for it and be passionate about what it is and does.

2. Make a list of all the chapter or section titles. As well as any other high points of the product, dramatic examples, bullet points in the advertising, etc.

3. Ask questions about each chapter, ad bullet point, and the notes you took. Just ask the question, and then be silent -- push the "mute" button if you have to. Again, the key to a good interview is not talking.

4. Get the person you are interviewing to tell a story to illustrate the answers to the questions you ask. You want to get people "lost" in their own stories -- revealing as much detail as they can. These stories will do a lot of the "selling" for you. Not only because it helps people fantasize about the benefits of what you're selling, but also because stories deliver  information in a way that makes it very easy for listeners to absorb everything.

5. Make a specific call to action at the end -- including a brief (but detailed) description of your hottest, most valuable bonuses. This is extremely important. Your interviews are "audio sales letters."  And like a text sales letter, they should have an offer and call to action. One reason people have trouble selling with audio interviews is they don't put a strong call to action or offer in it. You must do this. Your interview, if you do it right, will build desire and establish credibility for the product you are selling. But unless you give listeners a reason to act -- and act right now -- you will likely not get the sale.

6. Edit like crazy -- at least three "drafts."  If there's any magic to using audio, it's in how tightly you edit your interviews. Again, treat your audio interviews like actual sales letters. Would you send out the first draft of a paper or Internet sales letter?  Of course not. And it's the same with audio. You have to edit, edit, edit. Get rid of the "uhms" and "ahs" and anything that doesn't earn its place. The tighter and "faster" it sounds, the better. Just like with a regular sales letter, you want to have a fast-paced interview that fluidly goes from point to point to point -- with a strong call to action in the end.

Another thing is to keep your interviews to 60 minutes or less.

I learned this tip from Paul Hartunian (the publicity guy). He found during over 2,000  radio interviews that peoples' attention spans start to drift away after an hour.

So it's better to either do another interview (great excuse to bring people back!) or keep it to 60 minutes or less.

There's actually more to it than that, but that should get you going.

Another important point is to get your interviews transcribed.

Why?

Two reasons:

First reason is, while lots of people love listening to audio and hate reading, there are also a lot of people who won't listen to audio and would much rather read.

By having both audio and a written transcript, you are "upping" your chances of getting sales. I see it all the time with the interviews on my site. I wouldn't nab as many sales from my interviews if I didn't have them transcribed.

Another reason is because a good audio interview -- that delivers a lot of real value -- is like "search engine candy" when put in text form. You will start  automatically attracting search engine traffic you never would have gotten otherwise. Sometimes a lot of traffic.

You will also attract a ton of word-of-mouth traffic, too.

For example, I get hundreds of unique visitors per month just from referrals. People like to tell their friends, colleagues and newsletter subscribers about valuable information they find. This is a great way to tap into all that good will and free traffic.

Now, you can transcribe your interviews yourself or have someone else do it. If you outsource (and I recommend you do), I suggest staying away from the cheap services. Again, you get what you pay for.

And here's another thing:

Remember when I said sometimes it will not be possible to interview the product creator/author?

Well, the fact is, there will be times when you want to sell something as an affiliate despite not getting an interview with the author. Again, this goes back to sincerely caring about the people on your list -- like they're family. If you have the cure for some painful problem they have, you'd be doing them a disservice by not promoting it.

So what do you do in these cases?

The answer is simple:

Interview Yourself

For instance, not long ago I bought the license to sell Paul Hartunian's publicity  course.

But getting Paul to do an interview with me would likely take some major "string pulling", to say the least.

So what did I do?

I simply put on my own tele-seminar about how to write a press release instead.

This tele-seminar did everything I would have wanted in a real interview -- except I did all the work and had to prepare a little more for it.

By the way, does the thought of doing a live tele-seminar scare you?

Don't worry -- you can just as easily read from an outline into a digital recorder and turn that into an MP3.

See?  No problem.

Anyway, audio marketing is extremely powerful.

Use it and watch your conversions shoot right through the roof.

---

# Step Eight: Why You Should Almost Never Promote the Product You Are Selling... And What to Promote Instead

The following is going to go against the grain of what almost every other affiliate marketer does. But I suspect if people at least tested doing this, they would almost certainly see their sales and conversions skyrocket. I know I did.

You see, one of the things I do, that none of the people I've so far competed against do, is I almost never promote an affiliate link.

Instead, what I do -- and recommend you at least test doing -- is promote the interview with the author (or the interview you did with yourself).

Now, why would you do this?

Because most people hate being "sold" to. They are already being bombarded by some 3,000+ forms of advertising each day trying to persuade them to do this, that or the other thing.

In most cases, people are not looking for another sales pitch.

But They Are Looking for Solutions To Their Problems

And the trick here is to wrap your sales pitch up in something inherently valuable -- so it is not easily ignored, deleted or clicked away from.

Smart marketers have known for years that ads that look editorial -- instead of like "ads" -- almost always get much bigger responses.

This is why ads written by the truly great copywriters -- like Gary Bencivenga, Jim Rutz, David Deutsch, the late David Ogilvy, etc. -- never look, read or "feel" like sales pitches.

And this is also why, whenever I compete against other affiliate marketers, I almost always get higher conversions.

In other words, instead of "selling" people -- I let them buy.

If you only send your prospects to sales pitches, you will not only eventually be ignored, but you will probably be "black-balled" -- as they will likely start to assume everything you send them is just another "pitch" and act accordingly.

Granted, there are some people who can get away with this. And there are some markets where being a "super pitcher" works (certain biz opp. markets, for example, where people are impulsive and are always on the lookout for the next new money-making gimmick where they won't have to do any "work").

But in most cases, to just send one sales pitch after another is a huge mistake.   And I have found it FAR better to advertise your interview with the product creator, instead.

That way, you deliver value before asking for them to give you money. And when you do that, all kinds of wonderful things automatically happen.

For example:  Your prospects bond with you, trust you and feel like they know you (even if you've never met). This alone will have a huge impact on your sales and conversion rates.

Sometimes when I talk with people who have listened to interviews on my website at http://www.BenSettle.com they get almost "groupie" on me -- and act like they're talking to a celebrity. Michael Senoff (HardToFindSeminars.com) gets the same reaction when people call him -- as if he's some big movie star, and they can't believe they "got through" to him on the phone.

I'm not making fun of anyone, it's just the way it is. Heck, I'm the exact same way when I hear people being interviewed -- they automatically become a sort of "celebrity" in my mind.

And that's why, when you do this right, you become almost like a favorite radio talk show host sharing fascinating information with your audience -- and not just another "salesman" trying to crawl into their wallets.

Another thing that happens is you also set the law of reciprocity in motion.

This basically says that when you give something to someone, they feel a compelling urge to give you something back. And many times this "urge" is satisfied through buying what you are offering (especially if it's a reasonable offer for something they genuinely want).

Another (lesser known) reason to promote your audio interviews is because...

Most People Hate To Read

That may seem strange if you are a big reader.

But it's actually hard for most people to process and follow a written sales pitch.

In fact, those of us who do like reading are like "freaks of nature."  We're a very small minority.

Finally (and perhaps most important of all), by using audio interviews you are doing what the late (great) copywriter Eugene Schwartz did in his space ads:  You are temporarily "borrowing" the credibility of the media you are using.

This is extremely powerful. In his classic book, Breakthrough Advertising, Gene shows how making one of his ads that ran in the Wall Street Journal look exactly like a Wall Street Journal editorial column resulted in 19 times more orders than normal!

That's a big jump, to say the least.

And it's the same with your audio interviews.

When you do your interviews right, you wrap your sales pitch in "clothes" your prospects trust and are already conditioned to get information from (i.e. a radio or television talk show).

And that's why, instead of directly plugging your affiliate links (again, there are exceptions to this), it can be much more effective to plug your interviews instead.

Many times people will not only listen to your interviews... but (if you make them truly valuable) download and burn your interviews to CD's for when they are driving to work or fiddling around in their offices.

And each time they listen, they "re-sell" themselves on you, your product and your offer. I've seen this happen over and over again for all kinds of products and markets -- especially "high ticket" products that cost $1,000+

And you know what?

This is probably the one thing -- besides sincerely caring about my list, as if they were family members -- I do that almost none of my competition does.

And I believe it is what's mostly responsible for my higher conversion rates.

Now it's your turn to use this simple secret with your affiliate promotions.

---

# Step Nine: How to Spot the Best Prospects On Your List Before Mailing Affiliate Offers

As I mentioned earlier, the first product I sold via affiliate marketing using the methods in this book was John Angel's swipe file masterpiece, "The Copywriting Crash Course."  And before I told a soul about it, I did two things:

1. Interviewed John (as described in the previous steps).

2. Made a complimentary report -- about how to quickly create a swipe file -- I offered via a sign-up form on thesame page the interview was on.

Why did I do this?

Two reasons:

For one thing, the report is perfectly in "sync" with John's product. Anyone interested in building a swipe file is the ideal prospect for John's "how to use a swipe file" course. It's almost a "no-brainer" purchase for many people.

And secondly, by offering this report via auto-responder, I can capture the names and emails of those interested in swipe files, and keep pitching the course (usually via the interview we did) to those particularly targeted prospects over and over again.

Plus, to make things more "automated" (so I could mostly set-it-and-forget-it), I pre-loaded a year's worth of valuable swipe file messages (spaced out 1-3 weeks from each other) in that auto-responder series -- many of which sell John's course.

Not in an obnoxious way, of course (the ezine is 95% content).

But in a calm, strategic way. More like a drip from a faucet than a gush of water pouring onto them.

The result?

Again, at the time of this writing, I outsell all other affiliates for that course -- even the ones with bigger lists, name recognition and reach.

And one big reason for this is because I took a couple weeks to create a year's worth of follow-up emails offering more information they are already interested in (and not a bunch of sales pitches), with a soft sell for John's course at the end.

I have tracked this and noticed I get sales all throughout this email series -- from people reading the first email all the way to people on the 23rd email message.

And before you even ask... you don't have to spend a lot of time on these emails.

Just make them short, info-packed tips that are at least 90% information, with 10% or less sales pitch (and sometimes no sales pitch at all). In my case, some are several paragraphs long. While some are just a couple sentences with a link to a neat swipe file ad or resource.

You can also mix and match the call to action.

In other words, write some of your emails so they send people to your interview with the product creator while sending others directly to the affiliate link.

After all, if you've established a good relationship with your list (and have effectively pre-sold them on buying from you), and if you've created a report that is in perfect sync with the product, then there will be some people looking for the product.

Just mix, match and test what works for you.

Now, obviously this report idea is not always practical.

I certainly do not do this with all the products I sell via affiliate marketing. Just the ones where it makes sense for me to put the time and energy into creating a long series of follow ups. (For example, I do this with Paul Hartunian's PR  course as well -- but not with Ryan Healy's freelancer report, simply because I don't have enough content to drip on a list week after week for that particular subject.).

And if it makes sense with what you're selling, I highly suggest you test doing the same thing.

Not only can it work like gangbusters... but you'll also have a great sub-list of people to whom you can sell other related products. This sublist will contain all the best prospects for the kind of product you are selling (after all, they opted in to the list willingly).

And each time you find a new product to sell, it's mostly just a matter of repeating the steps in this course to sell those people additional related products (with additional back end sales -- making it very easy for you to rack up the profits).

Incidentally, some people will tell you to offer everything (even the interview) via a "squeeze" page -- to capture everyone's name and email -- to yield the best results.

And for a lot of people that is the case.

However, that has not always been the case for me. Sometimes it has, sometimes it hasn't.

So definitely test this to see which works best for you, your product and your list.

---

# Step Ten: What to Do with An Affiliate Product That Has A Weak Sales Letter

There may be times when you find the perfect product to sell, but the copy is either weak or not "talking" to your particular market.

In those cases I highly suggest writing your own copy or  hiring a copywriter to do it for you.

Frankly, if I didn't know how to write copy, or didn't have the budget to hire a decent copywriter, I would take John Angel's methods (see resources page at end of this book) and follow what he says. I suspect you'd get a far better response than from most copywriters you could hire, anyway.

But here's the problem:

In most cases, like with ClickBank products, your affiliate link will send people to the weak product sales letter, and not an order form. So, if you were to write your own ad, the order link would then send them to another (inferior) sales letter instead of directly to an order form.

As you can guess, this will kill your sales and profits.

So what can you do?

Well, that all depends on the affiliate program and how it's set up. It never hurts to ask the affiliate manager of the product you are selling if there is a way to set up your own, unique order link that takes people directly to an order form.

If you are selling a product on ClickBank,  I recently learned of a way where you can get around this problem from an extremely savvy affiliate marketer named Lynn Terry.

And, with her permission, I have reprinted her instructions here...

Reprinted with permission from Lynn Terry:  www.ClickNewz.com

ClickBank Affiliate Tip: Direct Link to Checkout

From:  www.clicknewz.com/1198/clickbank-affiliate-tip-direct-link-to-checkout

In today's Affiliate Marketing Webinar, I shared a tip on how to get around bad sales copy when you want to promote a great product through ClickBank. The trick is to write your own sales copy for the product, and then link an order button directly to the checkout page.

Typically, when you are promoting a ClickBank product as an affiliate you will use what they call a "hoplink" to the merchant's sales copy. That hoplink tracks the click-through's and you get credit for every sale in the form of commission.

In the rare case that you want to bypass the merchant's sales copy, you can set up a direct link to the checkout page that will still track your referrals. Here's how you do that…

clickbank.net/sell.cgi?YOURID_MERCHANTID/ProductNumber/ProductName

You will need to put the "http://" in front of that, and also make sure the link comes out all on one line - it breaks into two lines here on this page, but you want to make sure to create the link with no spaces or breaks.

There are four things you need to edit in that link:

- YOURID which is your ClickBank ID
- MERCHANTID the merchant's ClickBank ID
- ProductNumber
- ProductName

The product name and product number can be found in the merchant's original checkout link. What I usually do is view the merchant's sales copy page, then click View > Source in my browser. This brings up the source code for the page, and you can locate the order button and analyze their order link.

Obviously it is best to get the merchant's permission to link directly to the checkout from your own sales copy for their product.

You'll also want to test your link once you create it…

Put the order link on your web page, and then click on it to make sure that it works correctly. Once you get to the ClickBank checkout page, put in your zip code and then scroll down to the bottom of the payment page to make sure that your affiliate ID shows up at the very bottom. This is how you know the link works correctly, and and that it tracks you as the affiliate referrer.

By the way, Lynn's tip also comes in handy when you have a product catalog page and you want to send people directly to an order form.  One way you can do it is by placing the following under your product description:

Buy Now |  More Information  |  Free Lesson

The "buy now" would send them directly to the order form using Lynn's method. The "More Information" would send them to the sales letter. And the "Free Lesson" would send them to your interview. I got an immediate bump in sales with some of my products when I added this simple verbiage to my catalog page. And so I highly suggest you test doing the same thing.

Remember, everyone is at a different place in the "order cycle."  Some of your prospects are cold, some are warm, some are hot -- totally pre-sold and ready to buy.

With the cold prospects, it can be good to send them to your interviews. With warm prospects you can probably send them to a straight sales page (or the interview). But with hot prospects -- who want to buy now -- why mess around?  If they are ready to buy send 'em right to the order form.

Note: This is a relatively new approach I've taken and I'm still determining how effective it is. So far, it increases my sales but lowers my conversion rate. I will be keeping my affiliate marketing newsletter list (see the gray box below) updated on how it goes.

---

# Final Thoughts

Now we've come full circle.

What you have just read is the exact method I use when selling products as an affiliate, joint venture partner or licensee.

There's really no "genius" involved.

No hype, gimmicks or fake enthusiasm is required.

Not only can it nab you significantly more sales and conversions, but by doing things like creating valuable interviews with the authors of the products you sell and having them transcribed, you will start naturally and automatically attracting new website visitors, search engine links and word-of-mouth advertising at the same time.

I cannot stress enough that, of all the steps, step number #1 -- sincerely caring about your list, like you would your own mother or grandmother -- cannot be ignored if you use my methods.

When you care -- and I mean, really care -- about the people on your list (and not look at them like a "herd") -- they will sense it in your interviews, your sales letters, your emails and all your other communications. This will make people naturally trust you and want to do business with you.

From what I can tell, almost nobody does all the steps outlined in this book. Usually people will do one or two or three of them. But it's when you combine them all when the real "magic" happens.

And because so few people take the time to do these steps, when you do them, you'll stick out from your competitors like a sore thumb.

Finally, I'd like to give you some bonus tips and resources on the following pages.

These are supplementary. And apply mostly to product authors and publishers, rather than affiliates. But I think even if you don't yet sell your own products, you will find them quite valuable.

---

# Bonus Tips

1. Another Interview Secret

If you sell a product and have affiliates (or plan to) this idea can be extremely profitable for you.

Here's how it works:

Basically, you want to get yourself interviewed about your product by someone with authority and credibility who will endorse (even if unofficially) your product.

If you are being interviewed by someone who knows what they are doing, they will plug your product at the end. And when that happens, you will have a marketing "tool" that is not only pure gold for your sales... but for your affiliates, too.

Why?

Because you can simply take the audio recording of your interview, chop off the ending (where your product is about to be plugged) and just say something like,

"This ends my interview about (interview topic). For more information about (product), and how to (Unique Selling Proposition) go to (your affiliate's unique URL). Go there now and you will also get (your affiliate's unique bonuses).

See how that works?

This way, your affiliates can promote your product using your interview -- and use all that credibility and value from the audio format to help them promote your product.

Of course, you probably won't have time to do this for all your affiliates. I would recommend only doing it for your highest producers -- those few who are already successfully selling your product.

By the way, to be safe, make sure you check with the interviewer, and let them know you plan to record the interview for marketing purposes, and that you plan to chop off the ending to replace it with yours. Obviously, if they are an affiliate of yours, they will not agree to this. Also, there have been some new FTC guidelines about affiliate link disclosure, I recommend checking with a competent legal authority about this, too.

2. Grab Their Snail Mail Addresses

Although email is fast, free and still relatively "new" -- it is not nearly as valuable as a snail mail address.

And that's why, if you want to maximize your profits, you should collect your customer's snail mail addresses. This is one of the reasons offering unique bonuses is so important. On the page with your bonuses you can offer incentives for people to give you their snail mail addresses.

Incidentally, one great way to do this -- although you won't get as many names on your list -- is with a service like www.Kunaki.com.

Kunaki.com will:

1. Create CD's and DVD's for you dirt cheap (less than $2 at the time of this writing).

2. Take the orders for you, process the credit cards, and mail the CD/DVD to your customers.

3. Send you the customers' names and addresses.

For example, you could do an interview with an expert and offer it to your customers for "free" (they just pay the duplicating and shipping fee). Then send the customers to Kunaki.com's order form to claim it.

You set the price at the lowest you can set it -- which is their actual, hard cost to duplicate it price -- and your customer only pays that cost plus shipping and handling.  Then, after Kunaki.com has fulfilled the order, they send you the name and address of the customer.

Again, you obviously won't get as many snail mail names and addresses (since they have to pay for the shipping). But you will get some extremely qualified names that are much more likely to buy from you again if you send them a targeted offer via snail mail.

And, of course, make sure the audio you send them leads to your other affiliate interviews...

4. Get Physical

If you sell a book and want to hook up with the largest distributors on the planet, consider using a service like www.LightningSource.com.

LightingSource.com can get your eBooks distributed all over the world and, even better, publish it as a physical book for you and automatically get it on Amazon.com, BarnesAndNoble.com, etc. Just like with Kunaki.com, these distributors fulfill all the orders for you, and get you in front of a huge number of buyers you'd never reach otherwise (although they will not send you the customer's address).
