Kind of like if you were calling up your best friend and sharing some incredible news you know she'll want to hear about.

You want an example?

Okay. Let me "whip" up an example that will drive this point home. After you read this I think you'll see exactly what I'm talking about.

Let's say there's this woman named Jane Doe.

And Jane has this problem where she just can't seem to get involved with good men. It's always one failed relationship after another. She knows there's something wrong, but she can't figure out what it is. She seems to be attracted to all the jerks in her life but pushes away all the nice guys who would treat her like a queen. And one day, lo and behold, she reads an ad in her issue of Cosmopolitan that says,

"How to make sure you never get involved in another bad relationship ever again... guaranteed. Call 1-800-555-5555 and leave your name and address and we'll immediately send you more information."

And our friend Jane does as the ad says and, a few days later, she gets a letter Federal Expressed to her.

She opens the letter and it's a whopping 20 pages. And stapled to the top of the first page is a little packet of aspirin.

This is the first time in Jane's life someone has ever "Fed-Exed" a letter to her and it's sure as hell the first time anyone has ever sent her a letter with something attached to the top.

So obviously, she has to at least start reading this sucker to see what it's all about.

Who wouldn't?

It reads something like this:

Dear Jane,

As you can see, I have attached a couple of aspirin to this letter. I have done this for two reasons:

1. I have something extremely important to tell you and I wanted to make absolutely sure I would get your attention.

2. I know the guys you've been involved with have been giving you nothing but headaches. But don't worry, I'm going to show you how to make sure you never waste your time with a guy who will treat you like dirt ever again.

Here's what it's all about:

I know you're sick and tired of being with the wrong men.

It's very frightening to finally come to the conclusion that every single guy you go out with is totally wrong for you. They treat you like dirt. They take you for granted. Sometimes they might even be abusive to you.

In fact, I actually spent 10 solid years going from relationship to relationship, attracting all the wrong guys... while pushing away any guy who wanted to treat me well.

I'm not kidding.

I hope your situation isn't that bad.

Chances are it isn't.

But I can tell you exactly why this sort of thing can happen to us. And... why even very intelligent women will go from one bad relationship to the next... even though we know it's not good for us.

There are actually several reasons.

I don't have enough time to go over them all in this letter. But one often overlooked reason has to do with a chemical our bodies naturally produce called Oxytocin.

Yeah, I know, I didn't know what Oxytocin was either.

Until I did some research.

And here's what I found:

A woman's body produces this chemical... and when it does... it strengthens the bonding process between her and whoever prompted her body to produce it.

It happens when we fall in love. It happens when we spend time with our new baby. It happens whenever someone makes us "feel good" basically. There have even been studies where a woman just thinking about her husband raised the levels of Oxytocin in her body!

Men have this hormone too... only it's different for them than it is for us.

Why?

Because as women, we produce a lot more estrogen. And estrogen intensifies the Oxytocin. While testosterone (which men produce a lot of) actually dilutes the Oxytocin.

But here's the thing.

According to some recent studies, if a woman has multiple sexual partners, for example, this will lower her levels of Oxytocin which will in turn cripple her ability to bond. And when that happens, it makes it very hard for her to stay with just one person.

This may or may not apply to you. Like I said, there are several reasons why some women continually end up jumping from one failed relationship to the next.

But, in case you're curious, here's how Oxytocin fits into this…

And then the letter spends about 7 pages going over the exact chemical process that does etc. Then it reverts to a (somewhat disguised) "sales" pitch about a seminar that will go over all the different reasons women get involved with the wrong men.

The letter further explains how the seminar is being given near her home. It will show her how to get her life straightened out, etc. There are two or three full pages of nothing but benefit-dripping bullet points speaking directly to her needs and desires.

And it only costs $499.00 to attend. Plus... she can spread the cost out in 10 payments of $49.000 dollars if she wants. And if she walks out before the seminar is over--for any reason--she doesn't have to pay at all.

That's how confident the writer is about the power of the seminar. There's no risk at all.

She can't lose.

Oh yeah, there's also a "P.S." that explains if she's one of the first 100 people to sign up for the seminar, she'll get a free beauty makeover at the local salon and two free tickets to a taping of Oprah... etc.

Now, in this scenario--with this letter... being sent to this woman... at this moment in her life... with all these valuable offers--do you really think it matters how long the letter is?

Hell no!

She can't get enough of it. It is filling a need in her. In fact...

It Reads a Lot More Like a Page-Turning Story Than a "Sales" Letter!

In fact, Jane doesn't even feel like she's being "pitched" at all. Instead, she feels more like she's been educated about something of great importance to her.

Now, does all this mean your sales letter HAS to be long?

No. Just long enough.

It can be one page if that's all it takes (the highest grossing sales letter in history is just one page. It's been mailed to over 600,000,000 people and still going strong!).

Don't make it long just for the sake of it.

That would be a bad thing.

It just needs to make its case and make a complete presentation. Just like a real, live sales person would do. Any ad that's boring is too long, and any letter that doesn't tell the whole story is too short.

If it only takes 1 page to do this, fine. If it's ten pages, fine. I've heard of one sales letter that was 64 pages long--and generated a huge response!

But you know what most "advertising pros" will tell you? "Short and sweet every time." "Nobody wants to read a lot of copy." "You need to be more concerned with brand awareness than you do direct selling."

And you know what?

Most of my clients stubbornly insist their list of prospects are "different".

They don't read long stuff.

They are too busy to read all that compelling copy that's speaking directly to every one of their needs and desires.

They are just too darn "professional" to read anything that has more personality than a corporate memo.

They only want a one or two paragraph email at most. Or a one or two-page direct mail sales letter at most.

Yet... in the same breath... these people will also tell me they're "having a tough time" getting sales and can't "figure out" why.

Sigh.

---

# Introduction

Dear Friend,

I think you will find this short book one of the best resources you can ever get your hands on if you write copy of any kind - including direct mail, websites, emails, space ads, post cards, auto-responders, landing pages, squeeze pages or even classified ads.

Why?

Because it will show you how to quickly, easily and painlessly build a huge and massive swipe file of the highest-pulling ads in the history of advertising.

Ads you can study and adapt for your own purposes.

And the best part is most of these ads can be gotten for free or very little money. In most cases I am simply handing you these ads from my own files!

Now, before we get crackin', there is one thing you should know.

And that is, some of the links in this report lead to websites I have no control over. As of the time of this writing, all the links are working fine. But if you find a link that's broken then please let me know.

Okay, my friend. That's enough chit-chat for now.

Let's get it on!

Ben

---

# Resource #2: Old Comic Books

Old comic books are gold mines of great ads.

The psychology the copywriters used in those old direct response comic book ads is sheer brilliance.

They knew exactly how to get "inside" your head and persuade you to buy things you probably would never have even considered buying before.

Plus, they were usually able to do it in just two or three sentences!

The key is to only look at the older comic books (before 1990).

For some reason comic book ads after that started shifting into image ads and away from direct response.

Best part: They're cheap.

Unlike baseball cards, comic books tend to go down in value. And the really old ones - especially titles that didn't sell well - can many times be bought for mere pennies.

In fact, if you go to a comic book convention you can sometimes buy entire boxes of old comics - containing thousands of ads - for less than $50. You can read more about this at:

You can get hundreds of great comic book ads right now at these links:

(look under the "Ben Settle" section)

(Thanks to Yanik Silver for this one - I originally found this link on one of his sites)

---

# Resource #3: Newspaperarchive.com

I first heard about this incredible site from Michael Senoff a couple years ago.

Basically, this website has archived tens of millions of newspaper pages from publications across the U.S. These archives contain nearly every single newspaper story, article, editorial, obituary, Op-ed piece and... yes... advertisement ever published.

Simply go to:

... and you'll have access to some of the highest-pulling ads of the last 100 years. Including ads by Eugene Schwartz, Gary Halbert, Dan Kennedy, Maxwell Sackheim and all the other advertising "masters" of the past century.

You can learn how to use this incredible resource for finding swipe file ads at:

And here's another tip:

You can give yourself an "instant discount" on their membership fee simply by splitting the cost with other copywriters or marketers you know.

---

# Resource #4: Black Belt Magazine

Seems every great copywriter and marketer in the world likes to play in the martial arts market.

And that's good for you and me. Because every single month you'll find ads in Black Belt Magazine that are simply AWESOME. (I've destroyed many an issue of Black Belt Magazine ripping out all the ads!)

---

# Resource #5: Copywriter Interviews

In-depth interviews with top copywriters can be the most valuable copywriting training you can get.

Not only because of the writing and "mindset" secrets they drop... but because they often reveal the company names of other great copywriters and marketers.

Take, for example, the interview Clayton Makepeace did with Gary Bencivenga (see below).

Gary dropped the name "David Rosengarten" in that interview... which lead myself (and probably a lot of others) to track his site down and find one of the finest Gary Bencivenga ads ever (see http://bensettle.com/blog/remote-control-swipe-file)

This happens all the time in copywriting interviews.

Just make sure you read/listen to them closely, and write down any company names, headlines or other clues they drop about where you can find their ads (or other great copywriters' ads).

You'll find some truly outstanding swipe file ads this way you wouldn't otherwise be able to find.

---

# Resource #6: eBay

eBay is another swipe file gold mine.

You can find old comic books, magazines (i.e Black Belt) and even whole direct mail pieces on eBay!

Plus, you can also find other great swipe file books on there, too.

I suggest looking for Gary Halbert's collection of "57 Masterpiece Letters" and "10 Boilerplate Letters" - as soon as possible.

In my never-humble opinion, both are simply "must haves" for any copywriter.

---

# Bonus for You! A Sales Letter Analysis

Although you now have tons of swipe files to study, learn from, and select, I want to give to you a swipe file analysis that will help show you example how you might utilize such.

The following is a line-by-line analysis of one of my most successful sales letters ever. It made the client a ton of money, and not only can you read the ad for study, but each step of the way, I'll dive into the the psychology and mindset behind how it was written.

~ Ben

---

# How Even Skinny, "Barney Fife" Cops Single-Handedly Control And Dominate Violent Criminals, Gang-Bangers And Other Cold-Blooded Killers... Without Even Drawing Their Guns!

[BEN: Let's talk about the headline.  First of all, it combines curiosity and proof/credibility.  This is a super powerful combo to have in your headlines when possible.  Everyone knows cops are generally pretty tough so we get to use that credibility to our advantage here.  And anyone who has seen the show "Cops" will probably have wondered (as I often have) how these skinny guy cops manage to command respect and obedience from hardened criminals.  It also melts right into this market's head with the image of "Barney Fife."  This market is predominantly over 40 and will no-doubt be familiar with The Andy Griffith Show and Barney Fife's skinny, "wet noodle" body.  This adds to the curiosity factor and puts a great image with the claim.  And, of course, it also works in the ever-powerful "contrast" principle.  The idea of Barney Fife mixing it up (without a gun) with a violent criminal, gang-banger or cold-blooded killer has a nice psychological effect on the mind.  It's very hard for the brain to ignore contrasting ideas like this.  Anyway, it definitely helps to think like your market to come up with headlines that match this closely to how they think.]

From: Captain Chris
World Leader In Self Defense
9:27 am

[BEN: This was going to a house list, and so it's good to put who it's from in that case right at the top.  It's also good to insert a credibility element under the name.  In this case my client had - I believe - trademarked "World Leader in Self Defense."  But either way, you can claim a title whenever you want and it just adds more impact to all your claims.  In fact, the more you can "pepper" little credibility elements like that (even when it's not in the pitch itself like the "From" above) the stronger your ad will often be.]

Dear Friend,

If you want to know how even a "puny" man can control and dominate violent criminals (even a mob of violent criminals) barehanded... then here's how some of the world's most elite law enforcement agents do it.

And why you can do the same thing with the information in this letter.

[BEN:  Classic Gary Halbert "if/then" opening.  I like to spice mine up with something interesting other than just the claim.  In this case, I spiced it up with the word "puny."  Any kind of odd, out-of-the-ordinary word like that can add some much-needed flavor to a sentence.  Also notice, the credibility after the claim "here's how some of the world's most elite law enforcement agents do it..." - without that part, the claim would not be as believable or powerful.  More on the importance of believability in a second.]

Here's what it's all about:

[BEN: No time for dilly-dally, just jump right into the story after the lead.  I think most copywriters are killing their own profits by not doing this.  People tend to like to yap away, instead.  But the story is where the power is, so it's good to get to it as soon as possible.]

Several years ago one of my military contacts told me about this small (even puny), "Barney Fife" cop who got stuck in a small mob of angry rioters - some of them violent, and armed to the teeth with clubs, knives and even guns.

[BEN: Now we've set up the story and got the readers curious.  What in the world can a skinny, puny guy do if he gets stuck in a small mob of rioters?]

His partner had been knocked unconscious by a brick that flew through the passenger side window of his squad car.  And there was nobody else around to help.

So what did "Barney" do?

Wait for backup?

Drive away as fast as possible?

Nope.

After calling his position in to dispatch, this crazy cop walked into the thick of these violent criminals...

Without So Much
As Drawing His
Firearm or Tazer!

[BEN: Note the story - it creates tension, curiosity, and drama - this is a classic Hollywood type plot, and most people love to read stories like this.  This is one reason I'm big on watching lots of great TV and movies.  Anything with a good story will help you be a better copywriter.]

What happened?

[BEN: I figured this was the question on their minds...]

Within minutes, he had the entire crowd on their knees, hands on their heads, obeying his every command.  In fact, the way I heard it, he even had them raising their hands for permission when they wanted to ask him questions - terrified to disobey his orders.

[BEN: Is this so unbelievable?  I don't think so.  We are talking about cops here, and again, Hollywood has done a good job of making even the most average cop look like a tough guy over the years.  Notice the story only tells enough to get to the point, I didn't need to get detailed on how he commanded all this obedience, and if I had it would have slowed the momentum and given away too much information.  Information they can only get by ordering my product.  Of course, no mention of a product has even been made.  But this is how you set people up to want the product before you even introduce it - with a good story!]

Question is, how did he do it?

How did this droopy little man control this mob of lunatics ready to rip the head off of the first cop they saw?

[BEN: Again, these are questions the average reader is thinking as he's reading, you should always try to anticipate those questions and answer them head-on in your ad.]

The answer is actually pretty simple:

Many cops are, ounce for ounce, the most dangerous fighters in the world.

[BEN: The payoff.  This is a believable statement.  The story might have seemed impossible if it was some guy off the street.  But we're talking about a trained police officer.  The point is, at all times, your ad must be believable or people will click away.  And sometimes that means not leading with your biggest claims (another fact that gets lost on many of today's copywriters).  Remember this: Always go with what's more believable than what's more sexy.  Belief is a powerful emotion and people don't like to buy or listen to people who spout off things that are unbelievable.  Do you?]

I'm dead serious about this, too.

A lot of inner city cops I train see more combat in a month than most "hot-duty" soldiers see in a career.  And because of that, they are forced to learn, use and sometimes even invent (on the spot) ways to...

Inflict More Pain and Damage 
In a Single Second...
Than the Average Criminal Feels 
In His Entire Life!

[BEN: By the way... notice the subheads in this ad.  I very deliberately created a "double readership path" - which means you can get the main sales argument and gist of what the product is just by skimming up and down the pages and reading the subheads.  Make sure you do the same with your ads .]

This is no joke, either.

It's why you sometimes see even small, "wet noodle" cops command total cooperation from gang-bangers, rapists and even psychopathic killers with blood-stained weapons in their hands.

[BEN:  Again, note the drama, colorful use of words and descriptions.  The more you write and read, the easier this gets.  At first you have to do it consciously, but eventually it becomes second nature.  Just keep writing.  A good guide for this is Eugene Schwartz's book Breakthrough Advertising - he goes into a lot of detail about blending ideas and words together.]

And guess what?

If you would like to know how to put the fear of death and pain into anyone who dares mess with you and your family... and do it without long hours of training and sweating, or having to attend any self-defense schools... then I've got great news for you.

[BEN: Reiteration of benefits they want, just using different words, images and terminology.  But basically they are driven to be tough guys but are too busy and couldn't be bothered to learn it at a formal class or storefront dojo at the local plaza.]

Check this out:

For the past 15 years I've trained some of the toughest, "bad ass" law men in the world - like military police, inner city cops, secret service agents and other elite law enforcement agents whose lives are on the line every single day.

[BEN: Here we segue to Captain Chris's credentials.  The story, headline and talk about how cops are tough were great proof elements.  And in a second we're going to get into some hardcore bullet points.  To "prepare" the readers to believe them - as they are somewhat crazy sounding at first - I wanted to keep showing the credibility and proof.  Remember, it's ALL about belief.]

And recently, I held a private training with two cops showing them all the "ultra-violent" methods I teach law men (as well as some brutal "street smart" stuff some of them have taught me) and recorded it to DVD.

[BEN: A very simple way to introduce a product without all the hootenanny and fireworks copywriters tend to like to use.  I shamelessly pulled the term "ultra-violence" from the movie "A Clockwork Orange" - again, Hollywood is a gold mine of ideas you can use in ads...]

I call this DVD series: "Law Dog."

[BEN: I tried to convince the client to change the name to "Law Dawg" - especially since he's from New Jersey.  But nobody listens to me, haha.]

And it is specifically designed to let you break, hobble and dispense brutal (even inhumane) damage on men of any size, speed or disposition -

Even If You're a 
110 Pound "Bookworm" 
Who's Never So Much As 
Thrown a Punch Before!

[BEN: Again, another review of the main benefits, just using different words and terminology.  But it always adds up to the same benefit: Being a tough guy!  It also helps to mix up how you describe benefits like this as it keeps people from getting bored.  We are all so easily bored these days thanks to MTV and quick cutting TV shows and movies.]

I'm not screwing around here, either.

[BEN: Note also the persistent use of slang throughout the ad.  This is Captain Chris talking to his people.  They expect rough talk and appreciate a "tell-it-like-it-is" attitude.  Not all markets do, however, so be careful.]

These DVD's contain the exact same techniques multi-million dollar security firms, mercenary groups and other "military level" law enforcement professionals pay me a fortune (as much as $30k per day) to teach them.

[BEN: More proof before I spray the room with lots and lots of bullets. Also setting them up for the price later. The price will seem like a pittance compared to what the information is worth.]

Yet, if I hear from you right now, you can have these DVD's for just a small fraction of what they pay.

I'll tell you more about that in a second.

First, here's just a handful of the advanced fighting secrets on these DVD's:

[BEN: Now for the bullets... note how I'm constantly changing them up.  They aren't all "how to", they are deliberately positioned for variety.  I like to put the strongest bullets up front and at the end.  The reason why is because people who are in a hurry or who are skimmers are more likely to read the first several bullets and then the last few, so that's where all the fire power is.  Also pay attention to the use of imagery - very important.  You want people to "see" your benefits in their mind's eye.]

- Little-known, "dirty tricks" that let you practically destroy the skeletal structure in a man's wrists, arms or legs.

- A simple wrist lock so nightmarishly painful, he'll think you're sawing his hand off.

- How to slam anyone who "bum rushes" you on the pavement with so much speed and force his spine could very well shatter on impact.

[BEN: Again, note the images of pain?  These guys fantasize about kicking butt like this and being the hero if they are ever attacked on the street. Find out what your market fantasizes about and use those fantasies in your marketing.]

- How to break another man's arm on the spot in the heat of battle. (Great for when you're not feeling energetic and just want to end a fight - fast.)

- What to do if someone has you pinned to the ground, viciously trying to kill you. (This move is so blatantly nasty, I insist my police students make sure there are no video cameras around when doing it.  That way, the Internal Affairs bureaucrats can't accuse them of "excessive force" and take away their jobs.)

[BEN: More drama - and this market has a very health level of skepticism of all things government and bureaucrats.  Find out who your market distrusts and doesn't like and use it in your ads.  This market also despises liberals too... and so, the next bullet says...]

- A "magic bullet" fighting move that engulfs an attacker's nerve endings with white-hot pain - yet won't leave a scratch, bruise or mark on his body.  (This is how smart cops hide their "handy-work" from limp-wristed socialist judges and lawyers.)

- How to stretch and "pop" the neck tendons of anyone who tries choking you from behind. (He'll be in such excruciating pain he won't even remember his own name, much less want to attack you.)

- A simple "pain-inducement" secret used by cops to instantly stop perps jacked up on drugs or steroids. (This is one way small cops who weigh 100 pounds soaking wet control violent criminals. And it's a lot easier than you think.)

[BEN: When you get crazy with bullets - and bullets are the place to get crazy, so long as you are being truthful - it never hurts to throw in credibility elements whenever possible like the one above.  Also that bullet helps people understand the Barney Fife claim earlier.  Your entire ad is connected; make sure it's always consistent.]

- How to disrupt the neural pathways to another man's arms and legs - and literally "short circuit" his ability to move. (You will even hear the two officers I demonstrate this on in Law Dog moaning and gasping in pain.  This is some seriously violent fighting "technology" not 1 in 1000 self-defense experts knows about.)

- The scoop-and-toss secret that lets even skinny guys throw "Jabba the Hut" sized goons into walls or through windows.

- "Fool-proof" weapon disarmament tricks used by big city SWAT cops and anti-gang units.

[BEN: Here we have what I call an "expansion" bullet.  This is a break in the action to break up the sameness of the page.  It also lets you expand on an idea of a bullet and make the ad more interesting to read.  Note the "compression" bullets below - that is when you put a bunch of them in a single paragraph instead of in list form with an actual bullet point.]

And speaking of weapons...

I spend much time in Law Dog on firearm disarmament (as well as how to make sure nobody disarms you if you're packing heat).  Including...

The dumb mistake almost all men (even cops) make with their guns... Safest and easiest way to disarm a gangsta punk who threatens to "pop a cap in your ass"... What many firearms "experts" teach that is just plain wrong (and dangerous to you)... When you must never try to shoot a knife attacker... and even...

How To Snatch a Gun 
From Someone So Fast 
It Could Damn Near 
Rip the Trigger Finger 
Clean Off His Hand!

[BEN: C'mon now... who doesn't want to learn how to do that?]

You'll never see this "technique" on TV or in the movies.  And yet, it's probably the easiest (and most reliable) way to disarm a weapon ever discovered.

Here are a few more of the insider fighting tips in Law Dog...

[BEN: Now back to our regularly scheduled bullet points...]

- A forgotten, "judo illegal" way to injure (or even maim) someone who has you on your back.

- A very simple way to escape a vice-like headlock. (Doesn't matter how strong he is, either.)

- How to modulate the amount of pain you inflict during a fight.  (This lets you do anything you want to him - break his arm, toss him on his head, or just walk him to a nearby bench to sober up.  Good to use if you don't want to permanently hurt or cripple the guy.)

- The "neck crank" technique used by police to prevent criminals from taking their weapons when fighting hand to hand.

- How to use plain old gravity to temporarily keep a knife attacker at bay.

[BEN: Again, note how these bullets are varied - hard claims, curiosity, credibility, a mixture of them all... you always want to keep your reader on his toes looking forward to your next sentence all the way until the very end...]

- An unusually brutal way of throwing another man that's so effective, even trained experts are afraid of hurting their partners when practicing it.  (No matter how "gently" you do this, their heads will bounce off the mat, floor or pavement like a basketball.)

[BEN: This is what I call "precaution copy" - where you talk about a benefit or claim that is so powerful and effective you actually have to be careful using it.  You can see this in high pulling weight loss ads if you look carefully.  But I have found it endlessly useful for other kinds of products as well - such as when you are talking about fighting.]

- The filthy "back drag" move that lets you take down even experienced black belts (and other trained fighters) in mere seconds.

- A secret "sneak attack" move that gives you the element of surprise in any fight.

- The "far side dump" move that'll crack open your attacker's head on the asphalt like a coconut.

[BEN: Again, note the images, and pain and other emotions going on in these bullets.  You have to pour it on strong and make your writing interesting, and never boring.]

- An almost forgotten, "old timer" cop trick that lets you separate a man's vertebrae in a fight - giving you complete control over him.

- How to send any man who touches you airborne without using any brute strength or fancy tricks.

- The "nerve slam" secret (used by state champion wrestlers) to put someone who tries tackling you straight to the ground, face first on the concrete. (Maybe even crushing an eye orbit or smashing a few of his teeth in the process.)

[BEN: I'd be lying to you if I said writing this sales letter wasn't tremendous fun...]

- An extremely violent way to use someone's toes (even if they're wearing boots) to literally paralyze him with pain.

- An "almost magic" way to add size and bulk to your body in a ground fight. (It's almost like "hulking out" in a way - giving you an instant tactical advantage.)

- And a whole lot more.

Anyway, that's just a small "taste" of what awaits you in Law Dog.

[BEN: That "this is just a small taste of what you get" line is a great way to segue out of your bullets.  You can use the word "taste" or "sample" or whatever you want.  But it's short and to the point and lets you get right into the product description and offer.]

There's no theory or "fluff" in these DVD's.

Instead, you will find the most advanced "street combat" moves ever invented.  The exact same moves used every day by the world's most elite cops to tear through armed gang-bangers, rioters and other dangers criminals...

[BEN: More proof and credibility.  It's needed after all those bullets.  Ideally you want to "sandwich" your bullets between lots of proof and credibility.  And yes, a great offer and guarantee is good proof.]

Without Even 
Breaking A Sweat!

So how much does Law Dog cost?

[BEN: Another very simple and easy (and effective) segue - this time to the price.  A lot of people like to screw around at this point and keep trying to justify a high price.  But if you've done your job properly and used the right headline, story, bullets, proof/credibility etc you don't need to.  Just tell 'em what they get and how much.]

If I hear from you today (right now), I'll let you have Law Dog for just $247, or take advantage of our easy 5x payment plan.

That's a mere fraction of what my private students have paid for this exact same information. (In fact, I used to teach this under government contract only to federal and secret service agents for $30k each - double my military contractor rate.)

[BEN: This makes the price seem extremely low, doesn't it?]

However, you cannot dawdle on this.

Not 1% of the most elite cops on the planet possesses this information. And I usually only allow law enforcement pros to buy it. But I'm making a special exception for you, since you are on my private customer "hot list."

[BEN: Nothing gets done without a deadline.  I didn't have a hard deadline to play with, but you can always inject scarcity in there.  Especially if it's possible the product may be shelved - and you never know when you will want to shelve a product - I do it all the time for a variety of reasons - so it's always true in that sense.]

So don't put this off.

I'll be yanking this page down soon - maybe even by tonight.

Grab your copy now by clicking the "Add To Cart" button below:

$247 Today Only $187
(ORDER LINK)

Or Take Advantage of Our Convenient Payment Plan...

$247 Five Monthly Payments of Only $49.95
(ORDER LINK)

Fear No Man,

Captain Chris

[BEN: Can't forget the PS, can we?  In this case the PS is describing a bonus they get with the product.  Note the PS is a mini ad - for the bonus - itself.  If you can swing it, find bonuses that have more perceived value than the actual product and you almost can't lose...]

P.S.  One more thing...

If I hear from you right now (and if there are any left in stock) I'll also toss in a rare (and extremely valuable) World War 2 military police fighting secrets DVD.

These are some of the most violent tactics I've ever seen.

And some of it even freaks me out at times. (If that tells you something.)

[BEN: Again, precaution copy - so freaky it freaks even him out.]

In fact, they are so violent, you should keep this DVD out of reach of children until they're old enough to handle detailed training on snapping necks, dislocating arms and strangling the life out of another man with fighting tricks nobody teaches anymore.

Here are some of the secrets you'll learn when you watch this DVD:

- The only choke hold ever invented for which there is no known defense.  (This move is just damn spooky - even by my standards.  Frankly, I refuse to even practice this move anymore.  One "slip" and you can very easily end someone's life.)

[BEN: Even more precaution copy...]

- The easiest bone to break on the human body... and how to strike it to end any fight in 5 seconds or less.

- A secret "bear hug" defense that's so ruthless, there's a good chance he'll be pissing blood immediately afterwards.

[BEN: By the way, this bullet was a result of research.  Nowhere in the bonus video does it talk about this bear hug defense making someone piss blood.  But it just happens to cause damage to the kidneys which can cause someone to pass blood like that.  The great Eugene Schwartz was big on copywriters being knowledgeable about all kinds of stuff - even stuff outside their usual business - and this is one reason why.  It adds more depth to your writing and claims.]

- The "Austin Powers" striking secret that lets you injure, paralyze or (in some cases) even kill another man on contact.

- 5 "fool proof" knife attack defenses that work regardless of your experience or speed. (Ideal for dislocating the arms and breaking the wrists of any punk who corners you and your family with a blade.)

- The "leg smash" method designed break another man's leg or knee joint on contact. (A few months with the physical therapist and he might be able to walk with the help of a cane.)

- How to defend yourself against blunt weapons - like clubs, baseball bats or crowbars.

- Two easy-to-learn "arm breaks" designed to detach his bicep muscle or rip his arm clean out of the socket.

- 4 lethal choke hold defenses that can save your life. (Fact: You only have 14 seconds to escape a strangulation before death. Here's how to make sure it never happens to you.)

- What you must know about people who are drunk or strung out on drugs before fighting them.  (Many a "bad ass" has been beaten to a bloody pulp because they didn't know about this.)

[BEN: Again... notice the bullets are mixed up - some are pure claim, some curiosity, some proof, some dramatic some a mixture of them all. Also note the detail.  The more detail you can describe something in, the more believable and interesting it is.  And the more believable and interesting your copy, the more likely it will result in a sale.]

Again, here's the deal:

If we have any left in stock (we could run out of these by the end of today), this WW2 military police training DVD is yours free with Law Dog.

But you must hurry.

Once these rare, "vintage" bonus trainings are gone... they're gone forever:

[BEN: Here we have real scarcity - if all else fails, you can make your bonuses scarce like this.]

$247 Today Only $187
(ORDER LINK)

Or Take Advantage of Our Convenient Payment Plan...

$247 5 Monthly Payments of Only $49.95
(ORDER LINK)

Your Takeaway

Okay, a few more things about this sales letter.  I really did enjoy writing it and that was probably a big factor for its success.  Also, notice it had entertainment value?  Infotainment (cross between information and entertainment) is extremely powerful.  The entertainment gets and keeps their attention, and the information satisfy their "logical" side.  Something to keep in mind...

To get a video/audio version of this analysis, along with video/audio analysis of several other of my top selling sales letters, and the soup-to-nuts "slacker-friendly" system I have used to write ads that have made tens of millions of dollars in, check out:

~ Ben "CopySlacker" Settle
